stonetop.summary: A few minutes from Stonetop, near the base of the bluff, in the Great Wood.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body:
A few minutes from @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.6yScslDfqrcCQ6CJ]{Stonetop}, near the base of the bluff, in @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-journal.JournalEntry.6kt1b8ozEREDCi4k]{the Great Wood}. Wide, shallow, swift, lined with small stones. Folks come here to wash and fetch water, but rarely alone. It's not deep, but it's still a body of water.

  • The current's constant rush and murmur
  • Flat plain on either side, gravelly, dotted with saplings, weeds, wildflowers
  • Vague sense of dread, of being watched
  • Spring: cold snowmelt swelling the Stream
  • Summer: dragonflies, lightning bugs
  • Spring/Autumn: leaves in current
  • Winter: eerie stillness, icy surface

Tip: raise your voice a little, like you’re talking over background sound

pairs.prompt: What little ritual do villagers perform every time they cross the Stream?
How far have you followed the Stream?
What strange sight did you see on the way?
Which of you have encountered the Quiet Twins, the ghostly children who haunt the Stream?
What's the scariest, darkest story you've ever heard about the Stream?
Who do you think the Quiet Twins were?

sections.heading: Hooks
sections.body:
• A dangerous beast takes to watering near town.
  • The Stream runs dry, despite ample rains
  • Something mysterious (dangerous?) is deposited by the current.
  • Haunting songs are heard on misty days; hot-blooded youths are intent on finding the source (an entrancing Fae, like Blush-of-Dawn, page 112).

The Quiet Twins appear more frequently, urgently trying to warn of… something.

sections.heading: Inhabitants
sections.body:
Tip: shrink into your seat, to make yourself smaller, bug eyed, while mouthing gibberish.

A pair of ghostly children who haunt the Stream. Sightings are uncommon, but common enough.

sections.body:
